# Edge Case Hunter — skf-create-skill (SKF v1.0 review)

Findings only. Grouped by severity. Each: file:line, branch/condition, trigger, gap, suggested guard.

---

## BLOCKER

### 1. Missing `tier` field after override fails type check
**File:** `steps-c/step-01-load-brief.md:35`
**Branch:** "If `tier_override` is set and is a valid tier value... use it"
**Trigger:** `preferences.yaml` exists but `tier_override` is set to an invalid string (e.g. `"forge++"`, `null`, integer).
**Gap:** No explicit fall-through behavior is documented; control flow doesn't say "fall back to detected tier and warn." Downstream every step keys off `tier`.
**Guard:** "If invalid → log warning and use detected tier; never silently apply unknown tier value."

### 2. Source root mutated in §1, but `source_root: null` for docs-only never re-checked
**File:** `steps-c/step-01-load-brief.md:82` and `steps-c/step-03-extract.md:84` (§2a)
**Branch:** docs-only short-circuit sets `source_root: null`, but §2a is "Skip... if `source_type: "docs-only"`". §2b (Resolve Source Access) has no docs-only skip — only §3 (Check for Docs-Only Mode) does.
**Trigger:** docs-only brief reaches §2b; section unconditionally loads `sourceResolutionData` and runs Tag Resolution / Remote Source Resolution against `source_root: null`.
**Gap:** No "skip §2b if docs-only" guard. Tag listing / clone protocols will execute on null source.
**Guard:** Add at top of §2b: "If `source_type: "docs-only"`: skip §2b entirely — no source to resolve."

### 3. Batch loop checkpoint can desync `current_index`
**File:** `steps-c/step-01-load-brief.md:45-47` and `steps-c/step-08-report.md:94-104`
**Branch:** "If `batch_active: true` and `current_index` is valid"
**Trigger:** User invokes `--batch` with a different directory than the active checkpoint references; or `brief_list[current_index]` no longer exists on disk (deleted/moved).
**Gap:** "is valid" is undefined; no path-existence check, no comparison of new vs cached `brief_list`.
**Guard:** "Validate `current_index < len(brief_list)` AND `brief_list[current_index]` resolves to existing file; if either fails, reset checkpoint and re-discover."

### 4. Headless ecosystem-match auto-proceed has no log destination
**File:** `steps-c/step-02-ecosystem-check.md:73`
**Branch:** "GATE [default: P] — If `{headless_mode}` and match found: auto-proceed... log: ..."
**Trigger:** `{headless_mode}` true; ecosystem match found.
**Gap:** No mechanism specified for "log" — no file, no evidence-report hook in this step. Step 1 also says "log each auto-decision" with no sink. Audit trail silently lost.
**Guard:** "Append decision to `evidence-report.md` Auto-Decisions section; if evidence-report not yet created (this step runs pre-compile), buffer to context list `headless_decisions[]` for step-05 §7."

### 5. `gh release view` per-tag loop loses partial output on mid-loop crash
**File:** `steps-c/sub/step-03b-fetch-temporal.md:96-102`
**Branch:** "Iterate over every `tagName`... If `gh release view` fails for a specific tag, log a warning and skip"
**Trigger:** Process killed (Ctrl+C, OOM) mid-iteration after partial writes to in-memory buffer (file written only at end per the implied flow).
**Gap:** No incremental flush; staging file only assembled after loop.
**Guard:** "Append each release to `releases.md` immediately after fetch; if loop aborts, keep partial file."

### 6. Atomic-replace of QMD collection has gap
**File:** `steps-c/sub/step-03b-fetch-temporal.md:138-144` and `step-03c-fetch-docs.md:126`
**Branch:** "If a `{skill-name}-temporal` collection already exists, remove and recreate"
**Trigger:** `qmd collection remove` succeeds, then `qmd collection add` fails (disk, permissions, qmd crash).
**Gap:** No rollback. Forge-tier.yaml registry still lists the (now non-existent) collection if previously registered. Future `enrichment` searches will hit a missing collection.
**Guard:** "On `add` failure: also remove the registry entry; emit warning."

### 7. Symlink replacement is non-atomic and races concurrent forges
**File:** `steps-c/step-07-generate-artifacts.md:87-93`
**Branch:** "If the symlink already exists, remove it first and recreate"
**Trigger:** Two concurrent `skf-create-skill` runs for same skill (or one process being killed between `rm` and `ln`).
**Gap:** Window where `active` symlink does not exist; consumers reading `{skill_group}/active/{name}/` get ENOENT. Concurrent runs can also race the version directory creation.
**Guard:** "Use `ln -sfn {version} {tmp_link} && mv -T {tmp_link} {skill_group}/active` for atomic swap; acquire skill-name flock before §1."

### 8. Promoted brief write can corrupt brief on crash
**File:** `steps-c/step-03-extract.md:96`
**Branch:** "Write the amended brief back to disk immediately"
**Trigger:** Process killed during write; partial YAML on disk.
**Gap:** No write-temp-then-rename pattern specified; no backup.
**Guard:** "Write to `{path}.tmp` then `mv` atomically; copy original to `.bak` first."

---

## SERIOUS

### 9. Description guard normalization can mask intentional whitespace
**File:** `steps-c/step-06-validate.md:34, 44`
**Branch:** "Normalize whitespace for comparison... but do not ignore content differences" / "What does NOT count as divergence: whitespace-only differences"
**Trigger:** Tool replaces multi-paragraph description with single-line equivalent that normalizes to same string.
**Gap:** Boundary between "whitespace normalization" and "content differences" undefined for newline counts inside description.
**Guard:** "Compare token-stream (split by whitespace, compare lists) instead of normalized strings; record diff if token count differs."

### 10. `top_exports[]` empty when extraction yields zero exports
**File:** `steps-c/step-03-extract.md:304`, consumed at `sub/step-03b-fetch-temporal.md:120`
**Branch:** "For each function name in `top_exports[]` (up to 10)"
**Trigger:** Extraction of a tiny library / docs-only / extraction failure produces zero `top_exports`.
**Gap:** Loop is empty — no message, but `targeted-issues.md` still gets created empty; later "verify at least one file was written" still passes from generic fetches. No documented "skip targeted searches if top_exports empty" branch.
**Guard:** "If `top_exports` is empty: skip §3b targeted searches with note 'no top exports — skipping function-targeted searches'."

### 11. Unicode / spaces in skill name break path interpolation
**File:** `step-07-generate-artifacts.md:24-37`, `step-05-compile.md:31`
**Branch:** "`_bmad-output/{skill-name}/`", "`{skills_output_folder}/{name}/{version}/{name}/`"
**Trigger:** Brief `name` contains spaces, unicode, or path separators (`../`, `:`, etc.) despite kebab-case requirement (which is not enforced at load).
**Gap:** Step 1 §3 lists `name` as required but does not validate against a regex. `skill-check`'s regex enforced only at validation (step-06), after staging dir already created with bad name.
**Guard:** "In step-01 §3, validate `name` matches `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,63}$`; halt before creating any path."

### 12. Workspace clone path with unicode / non-ASCII repo names
**File:** `references/source-resolution-protocols.md:74-79`
**Branch:** URL parse to `{host}/{owner}/{repo}` and `{workspace_root}/repos/{host}/{owner}/{repo}/`
**Trigger:** GitHub repo name with `.`, `--`, mixed-case, or owner with unicode (rare but legal in some forks). Existing case-insensitive filesystem (macOS) hits collisions when two repos differ only in case.
**Gap:** No normalization rule; no collision detection.
**Guard:** "Lowercase host; reject paths containing `..`; on case-insensitive FS, append a content-hash suffix on collision."

### 13. CCC index reuse skips re-init when settings.yml changed externally
**File:** `steps-c/step-03-extract.md:154-166`
**Branch:** "If `{remote_clone_path}/.cocoindex_code/` already exists... skip steps 2-3"
**Trigger:** Existing index was created with different/incompatible exclusions; user added new build artifact dirs.
**Gap:** No version/compatibility check on existing settings.yml; no detection of "settings drift."
**Guard:** "Read existing settings.yml; if standard exclusions list missing any entry, append + force re-index."

### 14. Implicit tag resolution: zero matches sets HEAD silently in metadata
**File:** `references/source-resolution-protocols.md:42-44`
**Branch:** "Zero matches: warn... set `source_ref` to `HEAD`"
**Trigger:** `brief.version` set, no tags match.
**Gap:** Warning is shown, but no error flag is recorded into evidence-report — only the source-vs-brief mismatch later may surface it. Headless mode swallows the warning entirely.
**Guard:** "Append `tag_resolution: {status: 'fallback-head', requested: brief.version}` to evidence-report context; surface as an evidence warning."

### 15. Concurrent forge runs racing `forge-tier.yaml`
**File:** Multiple — `step-03b §4` (registry update), `step-03c §5b`, `step-07 §6/§6b`
**Branch:** Read-modify-write of `qmd_collections[]` and `ccc_index_registry[]`
**Trigger:** Two concurrent skill compilations both updating registry; last writer wins.
**Gap:** No locking mentioned anywhere.
**Guard:** "Use file lock on `forge-tier.yaml`; or read-CAS by checking mtime before write; or store as append-only log."

### 16. `gh search issues` uses interpolated function name without escaping
**File:** `steps-c/sub/step-03b-fetch-temporal.md:122-124`
**Branch:** `gh search issues --repo {owner}/{repo} "{function_name}"`
**Trigger:** Function name contains `"`, `$`, backticks, or `--` (unlikely but valid in some langs e.g. operator names like `<<`).
**Gap:** No quoting/escaping rule.
**Guard:** "Sanitize function_name to alphanumeric + `_` for query; or shell-escape via stdin pipe."

### 17. Component-library §3 user-confirmation gate has no headless path
**File:** `steps-c/step-03d-component-extraction.md:40-43, 79-86, 91-94`
**Branch:** "Confirm exclusion? [Y/n]" / "Is this the component registry? [Y/n]" / "[P] Provide / [S] Skip"
**Trigger:** `{headless_mode}` true; component-library extraction.
**Gap:** No `{headless_mode}` default-action specified for any of these three prompts. SKILL.md §17 says "All gates auto-resolve" — but this step never references the convention.
**Guard:** "Add 'GATE [default: Y] — if headless: auto-confirm and log' to each prompt."

### 18. Authoritative file scan loses promoted_docs on workflow restart
**File:** `steps-c/step-03-extract.md:91-97, 125`
**Branch:** "[P] Promote: ...add to in-context list `promoted_docs[]`... rebuild on each run by scanning amendments"
**Trigger:** First-time promotion completes, brief written, but workflow crashes before step-05 §6 writes provenance map. On resume, §2a re-reads amendments → correctly rebuilds. But what about a re-run that goes via different brief path? Or amendments file truncated mid-write?
**Gap:** No verification that amendment write succeeded before continuing extraction; no transactional boundary.
**Guard:** "After write, re-read amendments file and assert appended entry present; halt if not."

### 19. Subpage filter accepts arbitrary URLs from sitemap
**File:** `steps-c/sub/step-03c-fetch-docs.md:73-83`
**Branch:** "Attempt sitemap/map discovery... select the most relevant pages"
**Trigger:** Malicious or compromised doc site returns sitemap with off-domain URLs (e.g. attacker-controlled).
**Gap:** No same-origin enforcement on discovered subpages; T3 cited URL becomes attacker URL injected into provenance map.
**Guard:** "Filter discovered URLs to same registrable domain as root URL; reject cross-origin."

### 20. Body split forbids `--write` but doesn't enforce it programmatically
**File:** `steps-c/step-06-validate.md:100`
**Branch:** "FORBIDDEN: Running `npx skill-check split-body --write`"
**Trigger:** Agent under aggressive auto-fix mode, or future contributor copies pattern from elsewhere.
**Gap:** Prose-only forbidance; no test/checker.
**Guard:** "Add a step-06 §4 post-condition assertion: verify the original Tier 1 section count in SKILL.md matches pre-split count; halt if reduced."

### 21. Source commit capture races with workspace fetch on concurrent run
**File:** `references/source-resolution-protocols.md:131, 187`
**Branch:** "`git -C {workspace_repo_path} rev-parse HEAD`"
**Trigger:** Another forge in workspace mode just performed `git checkout FETCH_HEAD` between this run's checkout and rev-parse.
**Gap:** No lock around workspace per-repo operations; commit captured may not match files actually extracted.
**Guard:** "Acquire per-workspace-repo flock for the entire fetch+checkout+rev-parse+extract sequence."

### 22. Empty `source_type: source` brief without version + no tags
**File:** `references/source-resolution-protocols.md:7`
**Branch:** "When neither `brief.target_version` nor `brief.version` is set: skip tag resolution"
**Trigger:** Brief has no `version` field. Step 1 §3 lists `version` as required → halts. But what if the brief has `version: ""` (empty string after sanitization)?
**Gap:** "required" is documented but not validated for empty string / whitespace.
**Guard:** "Reject empty/whitespace `version`; halt with explicit message in §3."

### 23. tessl recovery loop: re-run can itself produce different `description_score`
**File:** `steps-c/step-06-validate.md:143`
**Branch:** "re-run... once. If the re-run produces `description_score == 100`, ... continue suggestion iteration"
**Trigger:** Re-run produces a third value (e.g. 95), neither failure nor success.
**Gap:** Branch explicitly handles `==100` vs "recovery fails," but not `<100 && != original`.
**Guard:** "Treat any re-run score < 100 as recovery failure with the original halt message."

### 24. Cleanup of ephemeral clone uses `cd` to project-root that may have changed
**File:** `references/source-resolution-protocols.md:170-172`
**Branch:** "Reset working directory first: `cd {project-root}` using the absolute path captured at workflow start"
**Trigger:** Workflow start never explicitly captures project-root cwd; relies on caller environment.
**Gap:** Step-01 doesn't capture/verify cwd. If a step changed cwd into `temp_path` for `ccc index`, and process killed before cleanup, `rm -rf {temp_path}` with cwd inside it on Linux deletes but agent harness may have stale cwd.
**Guard:** "In step-01 §1, capture and validate `cwd_at_start = pwd`; use it for cleanup; verify it exists before chdir."

### 25. Validation skip for missing `skill-check` doesn't degrade quality score in metadata
**File:** `steps-c/step-06-validate.md:50-53`
**Branch:** "If fails: Perform manual fallback... skill-check tool unavailable"
**Trigger:** No `npx`, no `npm`, offline.
**Gap:** Manual fallback in §3 only checks frontmatter; sections 4 (split), 5 (security), 6 (tessl) silently skipped. metadata.json `quality_score` field never set; no flag indicating "validation incomplete."
**Guard:** "When skill-check unavailable, set `metadata.validation_status: 'manual-only'`; mark all skipped checks in evidence-report."

---

## MINOR

### 26. ccc query truncation may strip non-ASCII boundary
**File:** `steps-c/sub/step-02b-ccc-discover.md:59`, `step-03 §2b step 4`
**Trigger:** `brief.scope` contains multi-byte chars; truncation at 80 "characters" ambiguous (bytes vs codepoints) splits in middle of char.
**Guard:** "Truncate by codepoints; document explicitly."

### 27. `gh auth status` exit 0 doesn't imply API access
**File:** `steps-c/sub/step-03b-fetch-temporal.md:28`
**Trigger:** Token authenticated but rate-limited / lacks scope for `repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues`.
**Guard:** "Probe with `gh api rate_limit` and minimum-scope read; degrade if scope missing."

### 28. Fresh-cache check uses creation date but ignores source mtime
**File:** `steps-c/sub/step-03b-fetch-temporal.md:50-58`
**Trigger:** Cache 6 days old but upstream had 50 PRs merged yesterday → user gets stale temporal context labelled fresh.
**Guard:** "Compare `last_pr_merged_at` from current API to cache; force refresh if newer."

### 29. metadata.json `tool_versions` resolution doesn't cache between steps
**File:** `steps-c/step-05-compile.md:119-133`
**Trigger:** Same `--version` calls re-run from scratch; expensive on slow systems.
**Guard:** "Resolve once in step-01, store in context."

### 30. Step-08 batch checkpoint write can race with step-01 read
**File:** `steps-c/step-08-report.md:94-104` ↔ `step-01-load-brief.md:45-47`
**Trigger:** Crash between `last_updated` write and chained step-01 load.
**Guard:** "Write checkpoint atomically (tmp+mv); step-01 should validate `last_updated` against batch start time."

### 31. `extraction_mode: "docs-only"` uses an empty inventory but Gate 2 still displays "Files scanned: 0 / Exports found: 0"
**File:** `steps-c/step-03-extract.md:316, 322-328`
**Trigger:** docs-only mode auto-proceeds past Gate 2 — but the displayed counts are misleading.
**Guard:** "In docs-only branch, suppress the counts table or substitute 'docs-only — no source extraction performed'."

### 32. Step-09 health-check resolution fails silently if `shared/health-check.md` missing
**File:** `steps-c/step-09-health-check.md:5, 23`
**Trigger:** SKF install corrupted; `shared/health-check.md` deleted by user.
**Gap:** "Load `{nextStepFile}`" with no existence check; agent may hallucinate continuation.
**Guard:** "Verify file exists; if missing, log warning to evidence-report and exit cleanly."

### 33. Description sanitization replaces `<` and `>` inside URLs
**File:** `steps-c/step-05-compile.md:59-69`
**Trigger:** Description contains a literal URL like `https://example.com/<id>` (rare but legal).
**Gap:** Replacement is unconditional; URL becomes `https://example.com/{id}`.
**Guard:** "Document the rewriting in description; or skip-replacement inside URL-shaped substrings (best-effort)."

### 34. Auto-detect demo patterns match files in `node_modules/`
**File:** `steps-c/step-03d-component-extraction.md:34-37`
**Trigger:** `examples/` inside vendored `node_modules/` matches detection patterns.
**Gap:** No exclusion for vendor dirs.
**Guard:** "Pre-filter against `node_modules/`, `vendor/`, `.git/` before applying demo patterns."

### 35. Promoted-doc content_hash drifts between §2a (live read) and step-05 §6 (re-read)
**File:** `step-03 §2a:93` and `step-05.md:152-156`
**Trigger:** File modified by user during the workflow (long-running Deep tier).
**Gap:** Hash captured early; if file changes before §6 emits provenance, the entry says "T1-low promoted-authoritative" with hash that no longer matches the file at output time.
**Guard:** "Re-hash at step-05 §6 emission time and warn if differs from §2a hash."

---

# Edge Case Hunter — skf-create-skill (SKF v1.0 review)

Findings only. Grouped by severity. Each: file:line, branch/condition, trigger, gap, suggested guard.

---

## BLOCKER

### 1. Missing `tier` field after override fails type check
**File:** `steps-c/step-01-load-brief.md:35`
**Branch:** "If `tier_override` is set and is a valid tier value... use it"
**Trigger:** `preferences.yaml` exists but `tier_override` is set to an invalid string (e.g. `"forge++"`, `null`, integer).
**Gap:** No explicit fall-through behavior is documented; control flow doesn't say "fall back to detected tier and warn." Downstream every step keys off `tier`.
**Guard:** "If invalid → log warning and use detected tier; never silently apply unknown tier value."

### 2. Source root mutated in §1, but `source_root: null` for docs-only never re-checked
**File:** `steps-c/step-01-load-brief.md:82` and `steps-c/step-03-extract.md:84` (§2a)
**Branch:** docs-only short-circuit sets `source_root: null`, but §2a is "Skip... if `source_type: "docs-only"`". §2b (Resolve Source Access) has no docs-only skip — only §3 (Check for Docs-Only Mode) does.
**Trigger:** docs-only brief reaches §2b; section unconditionally loads `sourceResolutionData` and runs Tag Resolution / Remote Source Resolution against `source_root: null`.
**Gap:** No "skip §2b if docs-only" guard. Tag listing / clone protocols will execute on null source.
**Guard:** Add at top of §2b: "If `source_type: "docs-only"`: skip §2b entirely — no source to resolve."

### 3. Batch loop checkpoint can desync `current_index`
**File:** `steps-c/step-01-load-brief.md:45-47` and `steps-c/step-08-report.md:94-104`
**Branch:** "If `batch_active: true` and `current_index` is valid"
**Trigger:** User invokes `--batch` with a different directory than the active checkpoint references; or `brief_list[current_index]` no longer exists on disk (deleted/moved).
**Gap:** "is valid" is undefined; no path-existence check, no comparison of new vs cached `brief_list`.
**Guard:** "Validate `current_index < len(brief_list)` AND `brief_list[current_index]` resolves to existing file; if either fails, reset checkpoint and re-discover."

### 4. Headless ecosystem-match auto-proceed has no log destination
**File:** `steps-c/step-02-ecosystem-check.md:73`
**Branch:** "GATE [default: P] — If `{headless_mode}` and match found: auto-proceed... log: ..."
**Trigger:** `{headless_mode}` true; ecosystem match found.
**Gap:** No mechanism specified for "log" — no file, no evidence-report hook in this step. Step 1 also says "log each auto-decision" with no sink. Audit trail silently lost.
**Guard:** "Append decision to `evidence-report.md` Auto-Decisions section; if evidence-report not yet created (this step runs pre-compile), buffer to context list `headless_decisions[]` for step-05 §7."

### 5. `gh release view` per-tag loop loses partial output on mid-loop crash
**File:** `steps-c/sub/step-03b-fetch-temporal.md:96-102`
**Branch:** "Iterate over every `tagName`... If `gh release view` fails for a specific tag, log a warning and skip"
**Trigger:** Process killed (Ctrl+C, OOM) mid-iteration after partial writes to in-memory buffer (file written only at end per the implied flow).
**Gap:** No incremental flush; staging file only assembled after loop.
**Guard:** "Append each release to `releases.md` immediately after fetch; if loop aborts, keep partial file."

### 6. Atomic-replace of QMD collection has gap
**File:** `steps-c/sub/step-03b-fetch-temporal.md:138-144` and `step-03c-fetch-docs.md:126`
**Branch:** "If a `{skill-name}-temporal` collection already exists, remove and recreate"
**Trigger:** `qmd collection remove` succeeds, then `qmd collection add` fails (disk, permissions, qmd crash).
**Gap:** No rollback. Forge-tier.yaml registry still lists the (now non-existent) collection if previously registered. Future `enrichment` searches will hit a missing collection.
**Guard:** "On `add` failure: also remove the registry entry; emit warning."

### 7. Symlink replacement is non-atomic and races concurrent forges
**File:** `steps-c/step-07-generate-artifacts.md:87-93`
**Branch:** "If the symlink already exists, remove it first and recreate"
**Trigger:** Two concurrent `skf-create-skill` runs for same skill (or one process being killed between `rm` and `ln`).
**Gap:** Window where `active` symlink does not exist; consumers reading `{skill_group}/active/{name}/` get ENOENT. Concurrent runs can also race the version directory creation.
**Guard:** "Use `ln -sfn {version} {tmp_link} && mv -T {tmp_link} {skill_group}/active` for atomic swap; acquire skill-name flock before §1."

### 8. Promoted brief write can corrupt brief on crash
**File:** `steps-c/step-03-extract.md:96`
**Branch:** "Write the amended brief back to disk immediately"
**Trigger:** Process killed during write; partial YAML on disk.
**Gap:** No write-temp-then-rename pattern specified; no backup.
**Guard:** "Write to `{path}.tmp` then `mv` atomically; copy original to `.bak` first."

---

## SERIOUS

### 9. Description guard normalization can mask intentional whitespace
**File:** `steps-c/step-06-validate.md:34, 44`
**Branch:** "Normalize whitespace for comparison... but do not ignore content differences" / "What does NOT count as divergence: whitespace-only differences"
**Trigger:** Tool replaces multi-paragraph description with single-line equivalent that normalizes to same string.
**Gap:** Boundary between "whitespace normalization" and "content differences" undefined for newline counts inside description.
**Guard:** "Compare token-stream (split by whitespace, compare lists) instead of normalized strings; record diff if token count differs."

### 10. `top_exports[]` empty when extraction yields zero exports
**File:** `steps-c/step-03-extract.md:304`, consumed at `sub/step-03b-fetch-temporal.md:120`
**Branch:** "For each function name in `top_exports[]` (up to 10)"
**Trigger:** Extraction of a tiny library / docs-only / extraction failure produces zero `top_exports`.
**Gap:** Loop is empty — no message, but `targeted-issues.md` still gets created empty; later "verify at least one file was written" still passes from generic fetches. No documented "skip targeted searches if top_exports empty" branch.
**Guard:** "If `top_exports` is empty: skip §3b targeted searches with note 'no top exports — skipping function-targeted searches'."

### 11. Unicode / spaces in skill name break path interpolation
**File:** `step-07-generate-artifacts.md:24-37`, `step-05-compile.md:31`
**Branch:** "`_bmad-output/{skill-name}/`", "`{skills_output_folder}/{name}/{version}/{name}/`"
**Trigger:** Brief `name` contains spaces, unicode, or path separators (`../`, `:`, etc.) despite kebab-case requirement (which is not enforced at load).
**Gap:** Step 1 §3 lists `name` as required but does not validate against a regex. `skill-check`'s regex enforced only at validation (step-06), after staging dir already created with bad name.
**Guard:** "In step-01 §3, validate `name` matches `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,63}$`; halt before creating any path."

### 12. Workspace clone path with unicode / non-ASCII repo names
**File:** `references/source-resolution-protocols.md:74-79`
**Branch:** URL parse to `{host}/{owner}/{repo}` and `{workspace_root}/repos/{host}/{owner}/{repo}/`
**Trigger:** GitHub repo name with `.`, `--`, mixed-case, or owner with unicode (rare but legal in some forks). Existing case-insensitive filesystem (macOS) hits collisions when two repos differ only in case.
**Gap:** No normalization rule; no collision detection.
**Guard:** "Lowercase host; reject paths containing `..`; on case-insensitive FS, append a content-hash suffix on collision."

### 13. CCC index reuse skips re-init when settings.yml changed externally
**File:** `steps-c/step-03-extract.md:154-166`
**Branch:** "If `{remote_clone_path}/.cocoindex_code/` already exists... skip steps 2-3"
**Trigger:** Existing index was created with different/incompatible exclusions; user added new build artifact dirs.
**Gap:** No version/compatibility check on existing settings.yml; no detection of "settings drift."
**Guard:** "Read existing settings.yml; if standard exclusions list missing any entry, append + force re-index."

### 14. Implicit tag resolution: zero matches sets HEAD silently in metadata
**File:** `references/source-resolution-protocols.md:42-44`
**Branch:** "Zero matches: warn... set `source_ref` to `HEAD`"
**Trigger:** `brief.version` set, no tags match.
**Gap:** Warning is shown, but no error flag is recorded into evidence-report — only the source-vs-brief mismatch later may surface it. Headless mode swallows the warning entirely.
**Guard:** "Append `tag_resolution: {status: 'fallback-head', requested: brief.version}` to evidence-report context; surface as an evidence warning."

### 15. Concurrent forge runs racing `forge-tier.yaml`
**File:** Multiple — `step-03b §4` (registry update), `step-03c §5b`, `step-07 §6/§6b`
**Branch:** Read-modify-write of `qmd_collections[]` and `ccc_index_registry[]`
**Trigger:** Two concurrent skill compilations both updating registry; last writer wins.
**Gap:** No locking mentioned anywhere.
**Guard:** "Use file lock on `forge-tier.yaml`; or read-CAS by checking mtime before write; or store as append-only log."

### 16. `gh search issues` uses interpolated function name without escaping
**File:** `steps-c/sub/step-03b-fetch-temporal.md:122-124`
**Branch:** `gh search issues --repo {owner}/{repo} "{function_name}"`
**Trigger:** Function name contains `"`, `$`, backticks, or `--` (unlikely but valid in some langs e.g. operator names like `<<`).
**Gap:** No quoting/escaping rule.
**Guard:** "Sanitize function_name to alphanumeric + `_` for query; or shell-escape via stdin pipe."

### 17. Component-library §3 user-confirmation gate has no headless path
**File:** `steps-c/step-03d-component-extraction.md:40-43, 79-86, 91-94`
**Branch:** "Confirm exclusion? [Y/n]" / "Is this the component registry? [Y/n]" / "[P] Provide / [S] Skip"
**Trigger:** `{headless_mode}` true; component-library extraction.
**Gap:** No `{headless_mode}` default-action specified for any of these three prompts. SKILL.md §17 says "All gates auto-resolve" — but this step never references the convention.
**Guard:** "Add 'GATE [default: Y] — if headless: auto-confirm and log' to each prompt."

### 18. Authoritative file scan loses promoted_docs on workflow restart
**File:** `steps-c/step-03-extract.md:91-97, 125`
**Branch:** "[P] Promote: ...add to in-context list `promoted_docs[]`... rebuild on each run by scanning amendments"
**Trigger:** First-time promotion completes, brief written, but workflow crashes before step-05 §6 writes provenance map. On resume, §2a re-reads amendments → correctly rebuilds. But what about a re-run that goes via different brief path? Or amendments file truncated mid-write?
**Gap:** No verification that amendment write succeeded before continuing extraction; no transactional boundary.
**Guard:** "After write, re-read amendments file and assert appended entry present; halt if not."

### 19. Subpage filter accepts arbitrary URLs from sitemap
**File:** `steps-c/sub/step-03c-fetch-docs.md:73-83`
**Branch:** "Attempt sitemap/map discovery... select the most relevant pages"
**Trigger:** Malicious or compromised doc site returns sitemap with off-domain URLs (e.g. attacker-controlled).
**Gap:** No same-origin enforcement on discovered subpages; T3 cited URL becomes attacker URL injected into provenance map.
**Guard:** "Filter discovered URLs to same registrable domain as root URL; reject cross-origin."

### 20. Body split forbids `--write` but doesn't enforce it programmatically
**File:** `steps-c/step-06-validate.md:100`
**Branch:** "FORBIDDEN: Running `npx skill-check split-body --write`"
**Trigger:** Agent under aggressive auto-fix mode, or future contributor copies pattern from elsewhere.
**Gap:** Prose-only forbidance; no test/checker.
**Guard:** "Add a step-06 §4 post-condition assertion: verify the original Tier 1 section count in SKILL.md matches pre-split count; halt if reduced."

### 21. Source commit capture races with workspace fetch on concurrent run
**File:** `references/source-resolution-protocols.md:131, 187`
**Branch:** "`git -C {workspace_repo_path} rev-parse HEAD`"
**Trigger:** Another forge in workspace mode just performed `git checkout FETCH_HEAD` between this run's checkout and rev-parse.
**Gap:** No lock around workspace per-repo operations; commit captured may not match files actually extracted.
**Guard:** "Acquire per-workspace-repo flock for the entire fetch+checkout+rev-parse+extract sequence."

### 22. Empty `source_type: source` brief without version + no tags
**File:** `references/source-resolution-protocols.md:7`
**Branch:** "When neither `brief.target_version` nor `brief.version` is set: skip tag resolution"
**Trigger:** Brief has no `version` field. Step 1 §3 lists `version` as required → halts. But what if the brief has `version: ""` (empty string after sanitization)?
**Gap:** "required" is documented but not validated for empty string / whitespace.
**Guard:** "Reject empty/whitespace `version`; halt with explicit message in §3."

### 23. tessl recovery loop: re-run can itself produce different `description_score`
**File:** `steps-c/step-06-validate.md:143`
**Branch:** "re-run... once. If the re-run produces `description_score == 100`, ... continue suggestion iteration"
**Trigger:** Re-run produces a third value (e.g. 95), neither failure nor success.
**Gap:** Branch explicitly handles `==100` vs "recovery fails," but not `<100 && != original`.
**Guard:** "Treat any re-run score < 100 as recovery failure with the original halt message."

### 24. Cleanup of ephemeral clone uses `cd` to project-root that may have changed
**File:** `references/source-resolution-protocols.md:170-172`
**Branch:** "Reset working directory first: `cd {project-root}` using the absolute path captured at workflow start"
**Trigger:** Workflow start never explicitly captures project-root cwd; relies on caller environment.
**Gap:** Step-01 doesn't capture/verify cwd. If a step changed cwd into `temp_path` for `ccc index`, and process killed before cleanup, `rm -rf {temp_path}` with cwd inside it on Linux deletes but agent harness may have stale cwd.
**Guard:** "In step-01 §1, capture and validate `cwd_at_start = pwd`; use it for cleanup; verify it exists before chdir."

### 25. Validation skip for missing `skill-check` doesn't degrade quality score in metadata
**File:** `steps-c/step-06-validate.md:50-53`
**Branch:** "If fails: Perform manual fallback... skill-check tool unavailable"
**Trigger:** No `npx`, no `npm`, offline.
**Gap:** Manual fallback in §3 only checks frontmatter; sections 4 (split), 5 (security), 6 (tessl) silently skipped. metadata.json `quality_score` field never set; no flag indicating "validation incomplete."
**Guard:** "When skill-check unavailable, set `metadata.validation_status: 'manual-only'`; mark all skipped checks in evidence-report."

# SKF v1.0 Ship Review — Synthesis

**Date:** 2026-04-13
**Scope:** AR + ECH on 4 top-risk skills (8 parallel reviews)
**Preceding gates:** `npm run quality` PASS (exit 0, 13/13 sub-steps)

---

## Top Ship-Blockers (cross-cutting themes)

### T1. False-PASS engineering (verification/testing tools that can't fail)
The skills that grade other skills can silently produce PASSes from fabricated or empty evidence. Cross-cuts `skf-verify-stack` (AR C1-C3), `skf-test-skill` (AR C2, C3; ECH B1, B2, S4-S5), `skf-create-stack-skill` (AR H1-H2, ECH S8).

- **verify-stack AR-C1:** step-03 invents `protocols` / `data_formats` fields that don't exist in metadata.json → hallucinated compatibility verdicts.
- **verify-stack AR-C3:** Verdict rubric allows "Verified" with zero hard evidence (Check 4 never required to pass).
- **test-skill AR-C2:** Quick tier + naive mode + neither external validator = single-category Export Coverage drives 100% of score against 80% threshold. Gate is illusory.
- **test-skill AR-C3:** Subagent JSON treated as ground truth with no schema check; parent never re-reads SKILL.md. Hallucinated exports → vacuous PASS.
- **test-skill ECH-B1:** Empty skill → `total_exports=0` → vacuous PASS (no division-by-zero guard).
- **test-skill ECH-B2:** Malformed frontmatter only "warns"; unloadable skills still PASS.

**Fix posture:** Add a minimum-evidence floor (`active_categories >= 2`; force `INCONCLUSIVE` otherwise). Schema-validate every subagent JSON response. Downgrade verdicts when evidence is inferred not declared.

### T2. No atomicity / no rollback on partial writes
Every skill that writes artifacts can leave the target corrupt on crash. Cross-cuts all four.

- **create-skill AR-H4, ECH-7,8:** `active` symlink `rm` + `ln` non-atomic; promoted-brief write non-transactional; workspace repo race conditions.
- **create-stack-skill AR-C2; ECH-B5, B6:** Step-07 writes 8 artifacts non-atomically; mid-write failure destroys prior good version and repoints `active` to broken state.
- **test-skill ECH-B4, B5:** Result contract written without temp+rename; parallel test runs collide on shared `outputFile`.
- **verify-stack AR-M1:** `previous_report` symlink resolution ambiguous → silent overwrite risk.

**Fix posture:** Mandate temp-dir + atomic rename pattern across all artifact writers. Add per-skill flock. Flip `active` symlink only after all files validated.

### T3. Path/tool resolution without probes
Skills reference scripts and tools at paths that may not exist, with no resolution algorithm.

- **create-skill AR-C1:** QMD MCP tool name `vector_search` may be stale; no probe against shipping MCP.
- **create-skill AR-C2:** `--no-security-scan` flag existence never verified.
- **test-skill AR-C1:** `frontmatterScript` path resolution between `_bmad/skf/` vs `src/` undefined; the script *exists* but is unfindable deterministically.
- **test-skill AR-H2 + ECH-B3:** `shared/health-check.md` resolution; `compute-score.py` availability.

**Fix posture:** Every external tool invocation gets an explicit probe (`command -v` or `--help | grep`) with documented fallback. Every relative-path reference gets an explicit resolution list with HALT on miss.

### T4. Windows / unicode / path-with-spaces unhandled
- **create-skill AR-H4:** Every `git -C {path}` breaks on paths containing spaces. `rm -rf {temp_path}` unquoted.
- **create-skill ECH-11, 12:** Unicode skill names / repo names; case-insensitive FS collisions.
- **create-stack-skill ECH-M5, M6, M9:** `project_name` with unicode; symlink on Windows.

**Fix posture:** Either mandate `"{path}"` quoting in all shell snippets or declare Linux/Mac-only at v1.0 and defer Windows support.

### T5. Terminology + schema drift between sibling skills
- **create-stack-skill ↔ verify-stack AR-H4:** Verdict tokens (`Verified|Plausible|Risky|Blocked`) and filename (`feasibility-report.md` vs `feasibility-report-{project_name}.md`) diverge between producer and consumer.
- **create-stack-skill AR-C1:** `confidence_tier` means different things in step-07 vs step-08.
- **create-skill AR-M6:** "Forge tier" overloaded (specific tier vs "any AST-capable tier").

**Fix posture:** Extract shared schema docs to `shared/references/`; add parser-version guards that fail loudly on unknown tokens.

### T6. Headless mode is incomplete
- **create-stack-skill ECH-B2, S2:** Headless vs preferences precedence; STOP-wait-for-user gates with no headless default.
- **create-skill ECH-4, 17:** Auto-decisions "logged" but no log sink; component-library prompts have no headless path.
- **test-skill AR-H2:** Headless auto-proceed swallows health-check-dispatched failure silently.
- **test-skill ECH-S7:** Headless does not exit non-zero on FAIL; CI pipelines chain to export-skill on broken skills.

**Fix posture:** Audit every interactive prompt for `GATE [default: X]` block. Exit codes: non-zero on FAIL in headless. Explicit precedence rules.

---

## Per-skill finding counts

| Skill | AR (CRIT / HIGH / MED) | ECH (BLOCK / SERIOUS / MINOR) |
|---|---|---|
| skf-create-skill | 3 / 7 / 7 | 8 / 17 / 10 |
| skf-create-stack-skill | 3 / 6 / 4 | 7 / 17 / 17 |
| skf-verify-stack | 3 / 6 / 5 | 4 / 7 / 6 |
| skf-test-skill | 3 / 4 / 5 | 6 / 9 / 7 |

## v1.0 Go/No-Go Recommendation

**No-go for v1.0 label unless T1 + T2 are resolved.** T1 is the mission-critical failure (false-pass in zero-hallucination tooling); T2 is ship-risk (corruption on crash).

T3, T5, T6 are aggressive post-1.0 patch candidates.
T4 is release-note-able (declare Linux/Mac only) or defer to 1.1.

**Suggested sequence:**
1. Close T1 findings across verify-stack and test-skill (highest user-visible risk).
2. Close T2 atomicity pattern in a single shared helper used by all writers.
3. Ship v1.0-rc1, run AR+ECH second pass, ship v1.0.

## Raw reports

See adjacent files per skill:
- `ar-skf-create-skill.md`, `ech-skf-create-skill.md`
- `ar-skf-create-stack-skill.md`, `ech-skf-create-stack-skill.md`
- `ar-skf-verify-stack.md`, `ech-skf-verify-stack.md`

All 8 reviews returned and synthesized. Ship review saved to `_bmad-output/ship-review-v1/SYNTHESIS.md`.

## SKF v1.0 Ship Review — Bottom Line

**Quality gate (scripts):** PASS ✓
**AR + ECH review (judgment):** Significant ship-blocking findings

### Finding totals (8 fresh-session reviews)

| Skill | AR (C/H/M) | ECH (B/S/M) |
|---|---|---|
| skf-create-skill | 3 / 7 / 7 | 8 / 17 / 10 |
| skf-create-stack-skill | 3 / 6 / 4 | 7 / 17 / 17 |
| skf-verify-stack | 3 / 6 / 5 | 4 / 7 / 6 |
| skf-test-skill | 3 / 4 / 5 | 6 / 9 / 7 |

### Six cross-cutting themes

1. **T1: False-PASS engineering** — verification/testing skills silently produce PASSes from fabricated or empty evidence (verify-stack invents `protocols`/`data_formats`; test-skill's single-category score can clear 80% threshold; subagent JSON never schema-validated). **Mission-critical for "zero hallucination tolerance" claim.**
2. **T2: No atomicity / rollback** — every artifact writer can corrupt on crash; `active` symlink flips non-atomically.
3. **T3: Path/tool probes missing** — QMD MCP tool names, `frontmatterScript` resolution, `--no-security-scan` flag — all referenced without probes.
4. **T4: Windows/unicode/spaces** — `git -C {path}` unquoted everywhere.
5. **T5: Schema drift** between sibling skills (verdict tokens, filenames, `confidence_tier` semantics).
6. **T6: Headless gaps** — no exit code on FAIL; missing `GATE [default: X]` blocks.

### Recommendation: **No-go for v1.0 label** until T1 + T2 resolved.

T1 directly contradicts the "zero hallucination" positioning. T2 risks silent corruption in the wild.

Suggested next steps:
1. Fix T1 across verify-stack + test-skill (minimum-evidence floor, subagent-JSON schema validation, require Check-4 for "Verified" verdict).
2. Extract a shared atomic-writer helper for T2.
3. Ship **v1.0-rc1**, run AR + ECH pass 2, then ship **v1.0**.

Raw reports are in `_bmad-output/ship-review-v1/`. Want me to draft the T1 fix plan (concrete diffs), or run editorial reviews next?

content.text:
Before I fire 4 parallel editing agents on ~120 findings, two things genuinely need your call:

**1. Scope discipline.** "Fix them all" across 4 skills = ~120 individual findings with conflicting tradeoffs (e.g., some add strictness, others ask for graceful fallback). My recommendation: fix **CRITICAL + BLOCKER + HIGH + SERIOUS** in this pass (~60 findings, covers T1-T3 + T5-T6), and triage MEDIUM/MINOR into a GitHub issue checklist for v1.1. Triaging doesn't mean ignoring — it means we don't let polish drag the ship.

**2. Two design decisions I shouldn't make unilaterally:**

- **T4 (Windows/unicode/paths):** declare Linux/Mac only in README + CI for v1.0, or invest the ~1 day to quote every `git -C {path}` and audit unicode handling? My recommendation: **declare Linux/Mac only, defer to v1.1** — your user base is overwhelmingly Unix and this removes a large surface of untested paths from v1.0 scope.
- **T2 (atomic-writer pattern):** put it in `src/shared/scripts/atomic-write.py` as a shared helper (all skills `python3 shared/scripts/atomic-write.py ...`), or inline the `tmp+rename+flock` pattern in each skill? My recommendation: **shared helper** — one place to fix bugs, consistent behavior across skills, matches existing `src/shared/scripts/` pattern.

If you say "yes" to both recommendations + CRITICAL-through-SERIOUS scope, I'll launch immediately: shared cross-cutting fixes first (T2 helper + T5 shared schema docs), then 4 parallel per-skill editing agents, then `npm run quality`, then editorial reviews. Fire?

# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.10"
# dependencies = []
# ///
"""SKF Validate Output — Validate skill package artifacts.

Validates SKILL.md frontmatter, context-snippet.md format, and metadata.json
schema against agentskills.io specification. Outputs JSON validation results.

CLI: python3 skf-validate-output.py <skill-package-dir>
     python3 skf-validate-output.py <skill-package-dir> --generated-by quick-skill
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def validate_frontmatter(content, skill_name=None):
    """Validate SKILL.md frontmatter. Returns list of issues."""
    issues = []

    # Check frontmatter delimiters
    if not content.startswith("---\n"):
        issues.append({"severity": "high", "field": "frontmatter", "message": "Missing opening --- delimiter"})
        return issues

    # Find closing --- on its own line (not a substring match inside YAML values)
    end_idx = -1
    for i, line in enumerate(content.split("\n")[1:], start=1):
        if line.rstrip() == "---":
            end_idx = sum(len(l) + 1 for l in content.split("\n")[:i])
            break
    if end_idx == -1:
        issues.append({"severity": "high", "field": "frontmatter", "message": "Missing closing --- delimiter"})
        return issues

input.content:
# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.10"
# dependencies = []
# ///
"""SKF Atomic Write — Crash-safe artifact writing for skill workflows.

Provides three CLI subcommands skills can invoke via bash to avoid
partial-write corruption and active-symlink races.

Subcommands:
  write      Stage content into <target>.skf-tmp, fsync, then rename to <target>.
             Content comes from stdin. Creates parent dirs as needed.

  stage-dir  Create <target>.skf-tmp/ as a staging directory (mkdir -p).
             Caller writes files into it, then calls commit-dir to atomically
             swap it into place as <target>/ (with prior target moved aside
             to <target>.skf-rollback-<pid> and removed on success).

  commit-dir Atomically swap <target>.skf-tmp/ into <target>/. If <target>/
             exists, move it to <target>.skf-rollback-<pid> first; on failure,
             restore. Supports rollback via --rollback to undo the most recent
             commit by restoring the rollback dir if still present.

  flip-link  Atomically update symlink <link> to point at <target> using
             the `ln -sfn tmp && mv -Tf tmp link` pattern (or equivalent via
             os.replace on the link path). Holds an flock on <link>.lock.

All subcommands are POSIX-focused. Windows is out of scope for v1.0 (declared
Linux/Mac-only in README).

Exit codes:
  0 on success
  1 on user error (bad args, missing input)
  2 on operation failure (disk full, permission, race-detected)

CLI examples:
  cat metadata.json | python3 skf-atomic-write.py write --target /path/to/metadata.json
  python3 skf-atomic-write.py stage-dir --target /path/to/1.0.0
  python3 skf-atomic-write.py commit-dir --target /path/to/1.0.0
  python3 skf-atomic-write.py flip-link --link /path/to/active --target 1.0.0
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import errno
import fcntl
import json
import os
import shutil
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def _die(code: int, message: str) -> None:
    print(json.dumps({"status": "error", "message": message}), file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(code)


def _ok(payload: dict) -> None:
    payload.setdefault("status", "ok")
    print(json.dumps(payload))


def cmd_write(target: Path) -> None:
    """Write stdin to target atomically via temp + rename."""
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read()
    target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    tmp = target.with_name(target.name + ".skf-tmp")
    try:
        fd = os.open(tmp, os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT | os.O_TRUNC, 0o644)
        try:
            os.write(fd, data)
            os.fsync(fd)
        finally:
            os.close(fd)
        os.replace(tmp, target)
    except OSError as e:
        if tmp.exists():
            try:
                tmp.unlink()
            except OSError:
                pass
        _die(2, f"atomic write failed: {e}")
    _ok({"wrote": str(target), "bytes": len(data)})


def cmd_stage_dir(target: Path) -> None:
    """Create <target>.skf-tmp/ staging directory (clean if present)."""
    staging = target.with_name(target.name + ".skf-tmp")
    if staging.exists():
        shutil.rmtree(staging)
    staging.mkdir(parents=True)
    _ok({"staging": str(staging)})


def cmd_commit_dir(target: Path, rollback: bool = False) -> None:
    """Swap <target>.skf-tmp/ into <target>/ atomically."""
    staging = target.with_name(target.name + ".skf-tmp")
    rollback_dir = target.with_name(target.name + f".skf-rollback-{os.getpid()}")

    if rollback:
        # Restore from rollback dir (most recent by pid is ambiguous — require explicit one)
        candidates = sorted(target.parent.glob(target.name + ".skf-rollback-*"))
        if not candidates:
            _die(1, f"no rollback dir for {target}")
        chosen = candidates[-1]
        if target.exists():
            shutil.rmtree(target)
        os.replace(chosen, target)
        _ok({"restored": str(target), "from": str(chosen)})
        return

    if not staging.is_dir():
        _die(1, f"staging dir missing: {staging}")

    prior_moved = False
    if target.exists():
        if target.is_symlink() or target.is_file():
            _die(2, f"target is not a directory: {target}")
        try:
            os.replace(target, rollback_dir)
            prior_moved = True
        except OSError as e:
            _die(2, f"failed to move prior target aside: {e}")

    try:
        os.replace(staging, target)
    except OSError as e:
        if prior_moved:
            try:
                os.replace(rollback_dir, target)
            except OSError:
                pass
        _die(2, f"commit swap failed: {e}")

    if prior_moved:
        try:
            shutil.rmtree(rollback_dir)
        except OSError:
            pass

    _ok({"committed": str(target)})


def cmd_flip_link(link: Path, target: str) -> None:
    """Atomically point <link> at <target> using rename-over-symlink pattern.

    target is the value of the symlink (may be relative, as is convention
    for `active -> 1.0.0`). Held lock on <link>.lock prevents concurrent flips.
    """
    lock_path = link.with_name(link.name + ".skf-lock")
    link.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # Refuse if existing <link> is not a symlink (avoid rm -rf of real dir)
    if link.exists() and not link.is_symlink():
        _die(2, f"refusing to replace non-symlink: {link}")

    lock_fd = os.open(lock_path, os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT, 0o644)
    try:
        try:
            fcntl.flock(lock_fd, fcntl.LOCK_EX | fcntl.LOCK_NB)
        except OSError as e:
            if e.errno in (errno.EAGAIN, errno.EACCES):
                _die(2, f"another process holds flip lock on {link}")
            raise

        tmp_link = link.with_name(link.name + ".skf-tmp-link")
        if tmp_link.exists() or tmp_link.is_symlink():
            tmp_link.unlink()
        os.symlink(target, tmp_link)
        os.replace(tmp_link, link)
    finally:
        try:
            fcntl.flock(lock_fd, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
        except OSError:
            pass
        os.close(lock_fd)

    _ok({"link": str(link), "points_to": target})


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__, formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter)
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)

    p_write = sub.add_parser("write", help="Atomic file write from stdin")
    p_write.add_argument("--target", type=Path, required=True)

    p_stage = sub.add_parser("stage-dir", help="Create staging directory")
    p_stage.add_argument("--target", type=Path, required=True)

    p_commit = sub.add_parser("commit-dir", help="Commit staging directory to target")
    p_commit.add_argument("--target", type=Path, required=True)
    p_commit.add_argument("--rollback", action="store_true", help="Restore from rollback dir instead of committing")

    p_flip = sub.add_parser("flip-link", help="Atomic symlink flip")
    p_flip.add_argument("--link", type=Path, required=True)
    p_flip.add_argument("--target", type=str, required=True)

    args = parser.parse_args()

    if args.cmd == "write":
        cmd_write(args.target)
    elif args.cmd == "stage-dir":
        cmd_stage_dir(args.target)
    elif args.cmd == "commit-dir":
        cmd_commit_dir(args.target, rollback=args.rollback)
    elif args.cmd == "flip-link":
        cmd_flip_link(args.link, args.target)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.10"
# dependencies = []
# ///
"""SKF Atomic Write — Crash-safe artifact writing for skill workflows.

Provides three CLI subcommands skills can invoke via bash to avoid
partial-write corruption and active-symlink races.

Subcommands:
  write      Stage content into <target>.skf-tmp, fsync, then rename to <target>.
             Content comes from stdin. Creates parent dirs as needed.

  stage-dir  Create <target>.skf-tmp/ as a staging directory (mkdir -p).
             Caller writes files into it, then calls commit-dir to atomically
             swap it into place as <target>/ (with prior target moved aside
             to <target>.skf-rollback-<pid> and removed on success).

  commit-dir Atomically swap <target>.skf-tmp/ into <target>/. If <target>/
             exists, move it to <target>.skf-rollback-<pid> first; on failure,
             restore. Supports rollback via --rollback to undo the most recent
             commit by restoring the rollback dir if still present.

  flip-link  Atomically update symlink <link> to point at <target> using
             the `ln -sfn tmp && mv -Tf tmp link` pattern (or equivalent via
             os.replace on the link path). Holds an flock on <link>.lock.

All subcommands are POSIX-focused. Windows is out of scope for v1.0 (declared
Linux/Mac-only in README).

Exit codes:
  0 on success
  1 on user error (bad args, missing input)
  2 on operation failure (disk full, permission, race-detected)

CLI examples:
  cat metadata.json | python3 skf-atomic-write.py write --target /path/to/metadata.json
  python3 skf-atomic-write.py stage-dir --target /path/to/1.0.0
  python3 skf-atomic-write.py commit-dir --target /path/to/1.0.0
  python3 skf-atomic-write.py flip-link --link /path/to/active --target 1.0.0
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import errno
import fcntl
import json
import os
import shutil
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def _die(code: int, message: str) -> None:
    print(json.dumps({"status": "error", "message": message}), file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(code)


def _ok(payload: dict) -> None:
    payload.setdefault("status", "ok")
    print(json.dumps(payload))


def cmd_write(target: Path) -> None:
    """Write stdin to target atomically via temp + rename."""
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read()
    target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    tmp = target.with_name(target.name + ".skf-tmp")
    try:
        fd = os.open(tmp, os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT | os.O_TRUNC, 0o644)
        try:
            os.write(fd, data)
            os.fsync(fd)
        finally:
            os.close(fd)
        os.replace(tmp, target)
    except OSError as e:
        if tmp.exists():
            try:
                tmp.unlink()
            except OSError:
                pass
        _die(2, f"atomic write failed: {e}")
    _ok({"wrote": str(target), "bytes": len(data)})


def cmd_stage_dir(target: Path) -> None:
    """Create <target>.skf-tmp/ staging directory (clean if present)."""
    staging = target.with_name(target.name + ".skf-tmp")
    if staging.exists():
        shutil.rmtree(staging)
    staging.mkdir(parents=True)
    _ok({"staging": str(staging)})


def cmd_commit_dir(target: Path, rollback: bool = False) -> None:
    """Swap <target>.skf-tmp/ into <target>/ atomically."""
    staging = target.with_name(target.name + ".skf-tmp")
    rollback_dir = target.with_name(target.name + f".skf-rollback-{os.getpid()}")

    if rollback:
        # Restore from rollback dir (most recent by pid is ambiguous — require explicit one)
        candidates = sorted(target.parent.glob(target.name + ".skf-rollback-*"))
        if not candidates:
            _die(1, f"no rollback dir for {target}")
        chosen = candidates[-1]
        if target.exists():
            shutil.rmtree(target)
        os.replace(chosen, target)
        _ok({"restored": str(target), "from": str(chosen)})
        return

    if not staging.is_dir():
        _die(1, f"staging dir missing: {staging}")

    prior_moved = False
    if target.exists():
        if target.is_symlink() or target.is_file():
            _die(2, f"target is not a directory: {target}")
        try:
            os.replace(target, rollback_dir)
            prior_moved = True
        except OSError as e:
            _die(2, f"failed to move prior target aside: {e}")

    try:
        os.replace(staging, target)
    except OSError as e:
        if prior_moved:
            try:
                os.replace(rollback_dir, target)
            except OSError:
                pass
        _die(2, f"commit swap failed: {e}")

    if prior_moved:
        try:
            shutil.rmtree(rollback_dir)
        except OSError:
            pass

    _ok({"committed": str(target)})


def cmd_flip_link(link: Path, target: str) -> None:
    """Atomically point <link> at <target> using rename-over-symlink pattern.

    target is the value of the symlink (may be relative, as is convention
    for `active -> 1.0.0`). Held lock on <link>.lock prevents concurrent flips.
    """
    lock_path = link.with_name(link.name + ".skf-lock")
    link.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # Refuse if existing <link> is not a symlink (avoid rm -rf of real dir)
    if link.exists() and not link.is_symlink():
        _die(2, f"refusing to replace non-symlink: {link}")

    lock_fd = os.open(lock_path, os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT, 0o644)
    try:
        try:
            fcntl.flock(lock_fd, fcntl.LOCK_EX | fcntl.LOCK_NB)
        except OSError as e:
            if e.errno in (errno.EAGAIN, errno.EACCES):
                _die(2, f"another process holds flip lock on {link}")
            raise

        tmp_link = link.with_name(link.name + ".skf-tmp-link")
        if tmp_link.exists() or tmp_link.is_symlink():
            tmp_link.unlink()
        os.symlink(target, tmp_link)
        os.replace(tmp_link, link)
    finally:
        try:
            fcntl.flock(lock_fd, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
        except OSError:
            pass
        os.close(lock_fd)

    _ok({"link": str(link), "points_to": target})


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__, formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter)
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)

    p_write = sub.add_parser("write", help="Atomic file write from stdin")
    p_write.add_argument("--target", type=Path, required=True)

    p_stage = sub.add_parser("stage-dir", help="Create staging directory")
    p_stage.add_argument("--target", type=Path, required=True)

    p_commit = sub.add_parser("commit-dir", help="Commit staging directory to target")
    p_commit.add_argument("--target", type=Path, required=True)
    p_commit.add_argument("--rollback", action="store_true", help="Restore from rollback dir instead of committing")

    p_flip = sub.add_parser("flip-link", help="Atomic symlink flip")
    p_flip.add_argument("--link", type=Path, required=True)
    p_flip.add_argument("--target", type=str, required=True)

    args = parser.parse_args()

    if args.cmd == "write":
        cmd_write(args.target)
    elif args.cmd == "stage-dir":
        cmd_stage_dir(args.target)
    elif args.cmd == "commit-dir":
        cmd_commit_dir(args.target, rollback=args.rollback)
    elif args.cmd == "flip-link":
        cmd_flip_link(args.link, args.target)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# Feasibility Report Schema

**Purpose:** Canonical contract shared between `skf-create-stack-skill` (consumer) and `skf-verify-stack` (producer). Any change here must be applied to both skills in lockstep.

## Filename

```
{forge_data_folder}/feasibility-report-{project_slug}-{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}.md
```

`{project_slug}` is the slugified `project_name` (lowercase, hyphens only, no unicode). A stable `feasibility-report-{project_slug}-latest.md` copy (not symlink) is written next to the timestamped file for pipeline consumers.

## Frontmatter (required)

```yaml
---
schemaVersion: "1.0"
reportType: feasibility
projectName: "{project_name}"
projectSlug: "{project_slug}"
generatedAt: "{ISO-8601 UTC}"
generatedBy: skf-verify-stack
overallVerdict: "FEASIBLE|CONDITIONALLY_FEASIBLE|NOT_FEASIBLE"
coveragePercentage: <0..100 integer>
pairsVerified: <non-negative integer>
pairsPlausible: <non-negative integer>
pairsRisky: <non-negative integer>
pairsBlocked: <non-negative integer>
recommendationCount: <non-negative integer>
prdAvailable: <true|false>
---
```

**Unknown `schemaVersion` MUST fail loudly in consumers — never silently proceed.** Consumers check `schemaVersion == "1.0"` and emit an explicit error if mismatched.

## Per-pair verdict tokens (case-sensitive)

Exactly one of:

| Token | Meaning | Required evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `Verified` | All compatibility checks pass with declared evidence in both skills | Documentation cross-reference (Check 4) MUST pass with literal substring/name citation; language + protocol + type checks all pass |
| `Plausible` | Checks pass but at least one relies on inferred rather than declared evidence | Language + protocol + type checks pass; Check 4 weak or missing |
| `Risky` | At least one check produced incompatibility that a workaround may resolve | Any single check fails; workaround cited in recommendation |
| `Blocked` | Fundamental incompatibility that cannot be worked around | Language mismatch, protocol mismatch with no bridge, or type mismatch with no adapter |

## Overall verdict tokens (case-sensitive)

Exactly one of `FEASIBLE`, `CONDITIONALLY_FEASIBLE`, `NOT_FEASIBLE`.

- `FEASIBLE` — 100% coverage AND zero Blocked pairs AND zero pairs with Check 4 missing.
- `NOT_FEASIBLE` — Any Blocked pair OR zero coverage.
- `CONDITIONALLY_FEASIBLE` — Everything else.

## Body section headings (required, in order)

```markdown
## Executive Summary
## Coverage Analysis
## Integration Verdicts
## Recommendations
## Evidence Sources
```

Consumers grep for `## Integration Verdicts` to locate the pair table. The table header is fixed:

```markdown
| lib_a | lib_b | verdict | rationale |
```

## Producer obligations (skf-verify-stack)

- Set `schemaVersion: "1.0"` in frontmatter.
- Never emit a verdict token outside the defined set.
- When Check 4 (documentation cross-reference) produces weak/missing evidence, cap the per-pair verdict at `Plausible`.
- When `coverage_percentage == 0`, force `overallVerdict: NOT_FEASIBLE` regardless of pair results.

## Consumer obligations (skf-create-stack-skill)

- Verify `schemaVersion == "1.0"`; halt with explicit error on mismatch.
- Treat any unknown verdict token as a hard error (do not silently drop or map).
- Use filename pattern above when referencing prior reports.

## Versioning policy

Any change to the verdict token set, frontmatter keys, or section headers is a schema-breaking change and MUST bump `schemaVersion`. Additive changes (new optional frontmatter keys) bump the minor version; breaking changes bump the major version.

2 3 4 5 # Skill Forge (SKF) 6 7 **Turn code and docs into instructions AI agents can actually follow.** 8 9 [![Quality & Validation](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/workflows/quality.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/workflows/quality.yaml) 10 [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/bmad-module-skill-forge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge) 11 [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) 12 [![BMAD Module](https://img.shields.io/badge/BMAD-module-blue)](https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD) 13 [![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-%3E%3D3.10-blue?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://www.python.org) 14 [![uv](https://img.shields.io/badge/uv-package%20manager-blueviolet?logo=uv)](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) 15 [![Docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-online-green)](https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge/) 16 [![Discord](https://img.shields.io/badge/Discord-Join%20Community-7289da?logo=discord&logoColor=white)](https://discord.gg/gk8jAdXWmj) 17 [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge?style=social)](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/stargazers) 18 19 *Skill Forge analyzes your code repositories, documentation, and developer discourse to build verified instruction files for AI agents. Every instruction links back to where it came from — nothing is made up.* 20 21 **If SKF helps your agent stop hallucinating, give it a ⭐ — it helps others find this tool.** 22 **If it saved you an afternoon, [grab me a coffee ☕](https://buymeacoffee.com/armelhbobdad) — it helps me keep forging.** 23 24
25 26 --- 27 28 ## The Problem 29 30 You ask an AI agent to use a library. It invents function names that don't exist. It guesses parameter types. You paste documentation into the context — it still gets details wrong. You write instructions by hand — they go stale the moment the code changes. 31 32 This isn't an edge case. It's the default experience. 33 34 ## How Skill Forge Fixes This 35 36 1. **Analyzes your sources** — extracts real function signatures, types, and patterns from code repositories, documentation websites, and developer discourse 37 2. **Compiles verified instruction files** — every instruction links to the exact file and line it came from 38 3. **Version-aware** — skills are stored per-version, so updating to v2.0 doesn't break your v1.x skill. Compatible with [skills.sh](https://skills.sh) and [npx skills](https://www.npmjs.com/package/skills) 39 4. **Manageable lifecycle** — rename skills and drop deprecated versions without manual file surgery. Transactional safety for destructive operations. 40 5. **Follows an open standard** — skills comply with the [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io) spec and work across Claude, Cursor, Copilot, and other AI agents 41 42 > **Why two output files?** Every skill SKF produces ships both `SKILL.md` (the full instruction set, loaded on trigger) and `context-snippet.md` (an 80–120 token always-on index injected into `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` / `.cursorrules`). Per [How It Works → Dual-Output Strategy](https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge/how-it-works/#dual-output-strategy), Vercel research shows passive context achieves a **100% pass rate vs. 79% for active skills loaded alone**. # Skill Forge (SKF) **Turn code and docs into instructions AI agents can actually follow.** [![Quality & Validation](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/workflows/quality.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/workflows/quality.yaml) [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/bmad-module-skill-forge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge) [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) [![BMAD Module](https://img.shields.io/badge/BMAD-module-blue)](https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD) [![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-%3E%3D3.10-blue?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://www.python.org) [![uv](https://img.shields.io/badge/uv-package%20manager-blueviolet?logo=uv)](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) [![Docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-online-green)](https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge/) [![Discord](https://img.shields.io/badge/Discord-Join%20Community-7289da?logo=discord&logoColor=white)](https://discord.gg/gk8jAdXWmj) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge?style=social)](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/stargazers) *Skill Forge analyzes your code repositories, documentation, and developer discourse to build verified instruction files for AI agents. Every instruction links back to where it came from — nothing is made up.* **If SKF helps your agent stop hallucinating, give it a ⭐ — it helps others find this tool.** **If it saved you an afternoon, [grab me a coffee ☕](https://buymeacoffee.com/armelhbobdad) — it helps me keep forging.**
--- ## The Problem You ask an AI agent to use a library. It invents function names that don't exist. It guesses parameter types. You paste documentation into the context — it still gets details wrong. You write instructions by hand — they go stale the moment the code changes. This isn't an edge case. It's the default experience. ## How Skill Forge Fixes This 1. **Analyzes your sources** — extracts real function signatures, types, and patterns from code repositories, documentation websites, and developer discourse 2. **Compiles verified instruction files** — every instruction links to the exact file and line it came from 3. **Version-aware** — skills are stored per-version, so updating to v2.0 doesn't break your v1.x skill. Compatible with [skills.sh](https://skills.sh) and [npx skills](https://www.npmjs.com/package/skills) 4. **Manageable lifecycle** — rename skills and drop deprecated versions without manual file surgery. Transactional safety for destructive operations. 5. **Follows an open standard** — skills comply with the [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io) spec and work across Claude, Cursor, Copilot, and other AI agents > **Why two output files?** Every skill SKF produces ships both `SKILL.md` (the full instruction set, loaded on trigger) and `context-snippet.md` (an 80–120 token always-on index injected into `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` / `.cursorrules`). Per [How It Works → Dual-Output Strategy](https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge/how-it-works/#dual-output-strategy), Vercel research shows passive context achieves a **100% pass rate vs. 79% for active skills loaded alone**. Both halves ship in every skill SKF compiles. ## Before vs After **Without SKF** — your agent guesses: ```python import cognee

## Install

Requires [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) >= 22, [Python](https://www.python.org/) >= 3.10, and [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) (Python package runner).

```bash
npx bmad-module-skill-forge install
```

You'll be prompted for project name, output folders, and IDE configuration. When the install completes, open your IDE and invoke `@Ferris SF` to confirm Ferris is reachable — it will report your detected tools and capability tier. See the [docs](https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge/getting-started/) for other install methods.

## Quick Start

1. **Set up your environment:** `@Ferris SF` *(Setup Forge)* — detects your tools and sets your capability tier
2. **Generate your first skill:** `@Ferris QS <package-name>` *(Quick Skill)* — creates a verified skill in under a minute
3. **Full quality path:** `@Ferris BS` *(Brief Skill)* → clear session → `@Ferris CS` *(Create Skill)* — brief first, then compile for maximum accuracy
4. **Pipeline mode:** `@Ferris forge <your-library>` — chains Brief → Create → Test → Export in one command

> **Tip:** Start a fresh conversation before each workflow (or use pipeline mode to chain them automatically). SKF workflows load significant context — clearing between them prevents interference.

See the [workflows docs](https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge/workflows/) for all available workflows, pipeline aliases, and headless mode.

## Install

**Supported platforms:** Linux and macOS. Windows is not supported in v1.0 — shell tooling, symlink handling, and path quoting assume POSIX semantics. WSL2 works well as a Windows workaround.

Requires [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) >= 22, [Python](https://www.python.org/) >= 3.10, and [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) (Python package runner).
